title: Extended Contract
description: Machine-readable schema for the extended capability contract described in the toolkit spec.

id.description: Semantic capability identifier in lowercase slash-delimited form such as payment/retry or auth/session/login.

version.description: Semantic version number for the contract using MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.

owner.description: The team or individual accountable for maintaining this contract.

description.description: Business intent for the capability: what it does and why it exists.

inputs.description: Declared inputs that the capability accepts, along with their business constraints.

outputs.description: Declared outputs that the capability returns, along with the guarantees each output provides.

non_goals.description: Explicit statements of what the capability does not do.

use_cases.description: Business intents or practitioner scenarios that this capability satisfies.

open_questions.description: Unresolved decisions that must be closed before the contract can be treated as authoritative.

dependencies.description: Capabilities that this capability depends on and the rationale for each dependency.

error_handling.description: Declared error conditions and the required response for each.

trust_zone.description: Boundary classification for callers of the capability.

inherited_constraints.description: Constraints inherited from company, organization, or project level decisions.

constraint_history.description: Historical notes capturing what was tried, what failed, and the lesson that must survive.

deprecation_timeline.description: ISO date for planned deprecation, or null if no deprecation is planned.

migration_path.description: What dependents must do if this contract is deprecated.

versioning_strategy.description: How this capability handles breaking, backward-compatible, and corrective changes.

version_history.description: Record of major contract versions and what changed in each one.

name.description: Input field name used by the capability.

type.description: Declared JSON-compatible type for the input.

required.description: Whether this input must be provided by callers.

constraints.description: Business constraint on this input and why that constraint exists.

name.description: Output field name produced by the capability.

type.description: Declared JSON-compatible type for the output.

guarantees.description: Guarantee this capability makes about the output value.

nonGoal.description: Single-sentence statement of what the capability explicitly does not do.

useCase.description: A practitioner-facing scenario that this capability serves.

openQuestion.description: A question that still needs resolution before the contract can be authoritative.

version.description: Version constraint that this capability requires from the dependency.

rationale.description: Why this capability depends on this dependency in this specific way.

response_time_p99_ms.description: Ninety-ninth percentile response time threshold in milliseconds.

throughput_rps.description: Sustained throughput target in requests per second.

notes.description: Context needed to interpret the performance thresholds.

required_response.description: Required behavior when the error occurs.

requests_per_minute.description: Allowed steady-state request rate per minute.

burst_allowance.description: Permitted short burst above the steady-state limit.

notes.description: Context for the rate limit values.

source.description: Where the inherited constraint comes from.

date.description: Date of the prior attempt or incident.

context.description: What was tried or what situation led to the lesson.

outcome.description: What happened as a result of the attempt.

lesson.description: What future maintainers must remember from the history.

version.description: Contract version that the history entry describes.

changed.description: What changed and why it changed.
